summary: Owner directives: boards 2-5 online with transparent per-board data sources, and home.linzezhang.com must work. Boards 2-4 live as radar feed streams (registry config/boards_v0_3.yaml = PARAM-ADP-1123 -> MODEL_SPEC parameter_count 1123; feedparser -> board_items; per-source health, 3 consecutive failures auto-disable; first real fetch 6/7 sources, 138 items, RSSHub route honestly degraded), board 5 aggregates; boards 2-4 stay out of the daily selection pool (separate proposal). Radar page shows source name/platform/website/method/officialness/health/counts/last-fetch per board. Machine-lock ripples: frozen 模型参数文件.md summary active_parameter_count 1105->1106, user-center chain page row 442 + counters. Adversarial review round applied: external feed links restricted to http/https (no javascript:/data: injection on radar), future-dated items dropped, source ids required unique, network I/O moved out of the SQLite write transaction, disabled_auto sources actually skipped (kill switch), radar quick-nav no longer IndexErrors on names without a separator, board5 aggregate located by status not position and honestly scoped to boards 2-4, homepage favicon/robots handled + pill wrap/color-scheme fixed, README deploy commands disambiguated for the two workers; an unauthorized bioRxiv test-promotion in the local DB was reverted to shadow with a receipt. 3 new boards protection tests (922 total green). .gitignore extended to ignore per-worker .wrangler caches (home/).

Owner directives: the four boards were incomplete, expand the data sources; and home.linzezhang.com is the Owner's personal homepage, give it back. Source expansion bumps the registry to boards-v03-2 (PARAM-ADP-1123, 44 RSS/API sources: board1 +arXiv cs.CL/cs.CV +medRxiv browse streams, board2 23 top-journal official RSS, board3 5 Google-News ministry streams +RSSHub route, board4 11 Fed/SEC/FTC/NIST/White-House +2 aggregates); every feed_url was live-probed, first real fetch pulled 43/44 sources = 1050 board_items, the RSSHub public instance stays rate-limited and auto-disabled; boards stay out of the daily selection pool. Homepage: the custom domain home.linzezhang.com was returned from the agent's stray 'home' worker to the Owner's own linze-home-hub worker (untouched), the stray worker deleted, and deploy/cloudflare/home/ removed from the repo; prior docs that claimed the agent deployed the homepage were corrected. 922 tests green. Adversarial review of this round flagged scaling costs from the 6x source growth; fixed in-round: added a source_id index, concurrent feed downloads (ThreadPoolExecutor, network off the DB thread; per-source timeout 12s → real fetch ~4.4s vs the old ~15min sequential worst case), per-board retention cap (BOARD_ITEMS_KEEP_PER_BOARD=300, rolling delete so board_items stays bounded), URL-dedup of radar display items (cross-listed arXiv papers), and a corrected items_total docstring. New retention/dedup protection test; 923 tests green.

Cloud-native rebuild Stage 1 per Owner directives (web is the primary system not a Mac mirror; all of arXiv; bioRxiv normal ingestion; all boards into daily selection; Owner chose the free Cloudflare rebuild). New deploy/cloudflare/worker_cloud.js + wrangler_cloud.jsonc + schema_cloud.sql: one adp-cloud Worker + one D1 run all five stages (fetch all-arXiv via OAI-PMH + bioRxiv + rotating board feeds -> cross-board selection with 8 weighted features and abstain 59.6 -> deterministic 8-section lesson -> recall -> vendored FSRS-6 scheduling) on a daily cron; pages read/write D1 directly and recall grades update FSRS immediately (no write-back queue). Free-tier ~50-subrequest cap handled by batching all D1 writes and rotating feeds. bioRxiv also promoted to normal ingestion in the live local system. Live-verified on adp-cloud.linzezhang35.workers.dev: arXiv 220 (econ/stat/cs all fields), bioRxiv 30, 217+ candidates, econ paper selected, recall scheduled next review 2026-07-18 (3-day interval) in D1. Deployed as a separate worker; adp.linzezhang.com and the local Python system are untouched until Stage 3. Known gap: board3 Google News blocked from datacenter IPs (degrades honestly; Stage 2 fix). 923 local tests still green.

Cloud-native rebuild Stage 2 (all in deploy/cloudflare/worker_cloud.js): ported the six-theme design language from base.html (warm/minimal/fresh/techno/cosmos/forest token groups + sidebar/topbar/dock nav via a data-nav attribute on <html> + a localStorage theme dropdown), live-verified theme switching (warm sidebar <-> cosmos dock render correctly). Replaced board3's datacenter-IP-blocked Google News with Chinese-media RSS that fetches from Cloudflare (people.com.cn politics/finance, chinanews scroll, sina China focus) - live-verified 75 clean non-garbled items. Switched feed rotation to per-board staleness (4 stalest per board per run) so every board refreshes within the free-tier subrequest cap; added charset-aware fetch (gb2312->gb18030) and orphan-source cleanup. Adversarial-review fixes applied: theme applied in <head> before first paint (no color/nav flash); localStorage reads guarded (works with storage blocked); evidence weight keyed on official flag so board3 http links are not penalized; auto-disabled sources self-heal (retry after 3 days) so board3 cannot go permanently dark; orphan cleanup protects items referenced by reviews/selections; dock no longer overlaps the footer; dark themes use color-scheme:dark for the native dropdown; topbar nav centers; and a sidebar selector bug (nav.nav-side vs aside.nav-side) was fixed. Post-fix run is 正常 (no degradation), board items b1 270/b2 121/b3 75/b4 140. Separate adp-cloud worker; adp.linzezhang.com untouched until Stage 3.

files_changed: arxiv-daily-push/用户中心/功能任务测试证据追踪链.md
summary: Cloud-native Stage 3 cutover + commercial feature/polish pass. Cutover: adp.linzezhang.com detached from adp-mirror and bound to the cloud-native adp-cloud worker (Owner: 'the site I check is adp.linzezhang.com'); all routes verified 200 on the real domain, independent of the Mac; old mirror worker + local tunnel/web LaunchAgents left dormant. Features (deploy/cloudflare/worker_cloud.js): learning-stats dashboard (streak/due/mastered/learning/recall-rate), guided review session /review (most-due card -> reveal -> 4-grade -> auto-advance), board browsing /board/:id with pagination, search /search, past-picks /history, item detail /item/:id, and study-any-item POST /api/study/:id. Polish: meta/og/theme-color/favicon, security headers (CSP/nosniff/referrer-policy), no-store APIs, themed 404/error pages, robots, aria. Adversarial-review hardening: safeHref restricts rendered hrefs to http/https (defense vs feed-injected javascript:/data:), jsStr escapes </ in inline-script item ids, and the search LIKE escape now escapes the backslash ESCAPE char. Also fixed two governance count drifts that slipped past Stage 1's lighter CI job and were caught by the local unittest suite: the frozen 模型参数文件.md active_parameter_count 1106->1107 and the user-center traceability chain page (row 443 for J5 + counters); the full-worktree root validator reports 0 errors. Live-verified end to end on adp.linzezhang.com. Second review pass hardening: the KEEP_PER_BOARD retention prune now protects items referenced by cn_reviews/cn_selections/cn_lessons (no more orphaned review cards / soft-404 item pages / inflated due count); the due vital only counts cards whose item still exists; streak and the once-per-day grade dedup bucket by the user's local day (UTC+8) instead of UTC; grade buttons debounce against double-click; /item and /board return real 404s; /favicon.ico serves the SVG icon; board5 count is the cross-board sum. All live-verified on adp.linzezhang.com.

ChatGPT deep-dive button + local cleanup, per Owner: "delete useless local info; add a jump to ChatGPT so it can traverse the whole web, think and search deeply, give me some surprises, and explain the corresponding content in a detailed, professional, comprehensive, deep way." Feature (deploy/cloudflare/worker_cloud.js): a per-item "让 ChatGPT 全网深度追问" button on the daily pick (today), item-detail (/item/:id), and review card (/review), plus a topic deep-dive on /search. The button opens chatgpt.com/?hints=search&q= with a Chinese prompt pre-filled from that item's title, authors, categories, original link, and abstract (sliced to 600 chars), instructing ChatGPT to: (1) do a live deep web search across related papers/reviews/official sources/latest progress with citable links, (2) reason deeply about the real problem, core method, key assumptions, genuine innovation, limits and controversies, (3) give a detailed, professional, comprehensive, deep explanation for someone who wants to truly learn it, with apt analogies, (4) surface surprises (unexpected links, counter-intuitive conclusions, overlooked angles), and (5) end with a what-to-read/do-next list. New helpers deepDivePrompt/deepDiveTopicPrompt/chatgptHref/deepDiveBtn and a .deep-btn style. Security: the rendered href is esc()'d; encodeURIComponent leaves only the single literal & (-> &) with no <, >, or " in the query, so there is no attribute-breakout or injection surface; target=_blank carries rel=noopener noreferrer. Worst-case URL ~6.2KB (within browser/ChatGPT limits). node --check clean; deployed adp-cloud (version 220d0953) and live-verified on adp.linzezhang.com across today/item/review/search with the decoded prompt correct. Local cleanup (operational; NO tracked-file change, not in files_changed): the retired mirror/tunnel architecture no longer backs adp.linzezhang.com after the Stage 3 cutover, so the dormant com.linze.adp.web (uvicorn 8787) and com.linze.adp.tunnel (cloudflared) LaunchAgents were unloaded and their installed plists, the ~/.cloudflared/adp-tunnel-token, the 38MB var/bin/cloudflared binary, and dead logs removed; the reinstall templates stay under deploy/cloudflare/launchd/ (fully reversible), and the site still serves 200 on every route afterward, confirming the local residue was genuinely unused.

Theme-selector self-heal hotfix. Owner: "the webpage's 6 themes are gone / still not restored." Reproduced in-browser on adp.linzezhang.com: the six-theme picker read localStorage 'adp-theme' and applied it WITHOUT validating, so a stale/invalid stored value (from an earlier build) or an Object.prototype key ('constructor','__proto__','toString','hasOwnProperty') set an invalid data-theme (no matching [data-theme] CSS -> page fell back to the default warm palette) and left the native <select> at selectedIndex -1 (blank switcher). To the Owner this looked exactly like all six themes had vanished and their chosen theme was not restored. Fix (deploy/cloudflare/worker_cloud.js): the no-flash HEAD_INIT and THEME_JS now validate the stored theme with Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(THEMES, s) (an isTheme() helper) and fall back to 'warm' unless it is one of the six; applyTheme also guards its argument and rewrites the corrected value to localStorage so a bad entry self-heals on first load. hasOwnProperty (not truthiness on THEMES[s]) is used specifically so inherited Object.prototype keys are rejected. node --check clean; deployed adp-cloud (29a6a3f7) and live-verified: stored 'aurora' and 'constructor' both self-heal to warm (switcher shows 暖纸学习, data-nav valid), and picking 森林河流 persists across page navigation. Governance: PARAM-ADP-1124 re-attested (v4->v5, no row/count change) since worker_cloud.js is the change source; registers + dashboards synced. Observed during testing that the in-app preview browser cached the page (cache-buster needed); the live HTML is served no-cache so a normal phone refresh delivers the fix.

Restored the six-theme ambient MOTION layers. Owner: "the 6 themes just change one color now, the original motion/animation design is all gone -- don't fix A and lose B." Correct: the Stage 2 six-theme port into deploy/cloudflare/worker_cloud.js had copied only the colour tokens + layout and dropped every per-theme animated layer defined in the local design source src/adp/templates/base.html. Restored faithfully as pure CSS/SVG (no external dependency, CSP-safe): a THEME_FX map + a data-fx attribute (set in the no-flash HEAD_INIT and in applyTheme, with the same validate/self-heal discipline as the theme guard) drives fixed aria-hidden background layers -- cosmos: galaxy (drifting light band, 3 nebula glows, 2 twinkling star fields, 11s meteor); techno: 3 drifting white fluid clouds; minimal: ocean top-light + deep radial vignette; forest: layered hill-slope SVG + water band -- plus a .card fade-in entrance (frise keyframe) and a prefers-reduced-motion guard that disables all motion. warm/fresh stay clean (fx=none) as the original design intends. The external CloudFront hero videos in base.html were intentionally NOT ported (CSP + dependency fragility; the CSS ambient layers carry the motion). Content sits above the fx via z-index (fx at z-index 0, pointer-events none; main/nav/footer raised). node --check clean; deployed adp-cloud (99752091) and live-verified per theme on adp.linzezhang.com at both desktop and phone width (cosmos stars+nebulae, techno clouds, minimal ocean depth, forest slopes), with the ChatGPT deep-dive button and the theme self-heal intact. Governance: PARAM-ADP-1124 re-attested (v5->v6, no count change); registers + dashboards synced.

Made the theme motion boldly visible AND fixed the cache that was hiding every deploy from the Owner. After the previous change the Owner still saw no motion on computer and phone even on a fresh URL. DOM inspection (getComputedStyle/elementFromPoint) proved the fx layer rendered (display block, full viewport, stars gradient present) but two things hid it. (1) THE REAL BLOCKER -- cache: the browser served a fully cached page (computed card bg still 0.58, band 90s: the OLD css) despite cache-control no-cache; these browsers store no-cache responses and skip revalidation, so the Owner never received any recent deploy. Fixed: htmlResp cache-control no-cache -> no-store, must-revalidate (browsers always refetch; confirmed live via curl). (2) Too faint: the original prominent motion was full-screen hero videos (not ported); the remaining CSS ambient layer was near-invisible. Amplified the self-contained fx: cosmos gets on-screen nebula-glow backgrounds + a conic aurora sweep (banddrift 22s) + brighter/denser twinkling stars + a 6s glowing meteor, with cosmos .card made translucent (rgba 0.40) so the galaxy shows through; techno clouds enlarged/brightened; minimal gains a moving light shaft; forest slopes raised to 210px with a slow sway. Verified in a FRESH no-cache browser tab (computed card 0.40, band 22s, nebula bg present): the cosmos galaxy (bright star field + violet/blue nebulae) and techno clouds are now unmistakable at desktop width. Deployed adp-cloud (d5890974). Governance: PARAM-ADP-1124 re-attested (v6->v7, no count change); registers + dashboards synced.

Restored the today-page HERO first-screen -- the actual original theme motion. The Owner supplied the real v1.1 design package and said the original motion was still missing after ten changes. The spec made the miss clear: the signature motion was never the ambient CSS layer I kept tuning; it is the today-page hero first-screen, which the cloud port dropped entirely. Restored into deploy/cloudflare/worker_cloud.js per the v1.1 first-screen/nav contract: a THEME_HERO map + data-hero attribute drive, on the today page, a full-screen hero VIDEO for minimal/techno/forest (the three still-live CloudFront reference clips Velorah / Space Voyage / Aethera) with eyebrow -> giant serif title (today pick; per-char BlurText for techno) -> subtitle -> glass CTA over a per-theme gradient mask, and for cosmos the knowledge-vitals DASHBOARD (SVG ring gauge counting 000 -> today-score/104, STREAK/RETENTION/REVIEW-DEBT grid, 7-pick sparkline) over the galaxy (its NOVA video is 403 dead). warm/fresh stay clean. Video handling follows the v1.1 red-lines: src assigned by JS, muted/loop explicit booleans, play().catch plus an oncanplay handler so it never sticks on a frozen first frame (the exact failure red-line 1 warns about); added a media-src CSP entry for the CloudFront host. node --check clean; deployed adp-cloud (905513ff) and live-verified per theme in a FRESH no-cache tab: minimal/techno/forest videos actually play (paused=false, currentTime advancing, videoWidth>0) and the cosmos gauge animated to 094 with vitals/sparkline. Also fixed a wiring bug where the normal-day return path passed no hero. Caveat for the Owner: the reference videos are external CloudFront and one is already dead, so they should be moved to owner R2 storage for durability (v1.1 red-line 4). Governance: PARAM-ADP-1124 re-attested (v7->v8, no count change); registers + dashboards synced.

Self-hosted the hero videos on the Worker to remove the fragile external CloudFront dependency (Owner fully authorized, but does not want to touch the dashboard). R2 -- the v1.1 red-line 4 recommendation -- needs a one-time Cloudflare dashboard enable, which is a billing/terms action not taken on the Owner behalf; used Cloudflare Workers static assets instead: free, same-origin, durable, no dashboard step. Downloaded the three still-live reference clips (Velorah/Space-Voyage/Aethera) and compressed them with ffmpeg (obtained via the pip package imageio-ffmpeg, no system install) to 1280x720 H.264 no-audio faststart loops: 62MB -> 7MB total (velorah 1.5MB, voyage 1.9MB, aethera 2.5MB), also far lighter on mobile. Stored under deploy/cloudflare/assets/media, added a wrangler assets binding (assets.directory ./assets), pointed HERO_VIDEO at same-origin /media/*.mp4, and tightened the CSP media-src back to self (CloudFront removed entirely -> zero external media dependency). The 7MB of mp4 were force-added past the arxiv-daily-push/.gitignore media/ rule because durability requires them versioned (no live source remains to regenerate from; CLAUDE.md permits binaries when the task needs them). Deployed adp-cloud (assets uploaded, 3 files) and live-verified: /media/velorah.mp4 /voyage.mp4 /aethera.mp4 all serve 200 video/mp4 same-origin, the response CSP media-src is now self only, and minimal plays from /media/velorah.mp4 (paused=false, currentTime advancing, videoWidth 1280). cosmos keeps the dashboard (its NOVA clip was already 403). Governance: PARAM-ADP-1124 re-attested (v8->v9, no count change); registers + dashboards synced.

Freeze DIR-007, the free-tier hard budget, after the Owner enabled Cloudflare R2. The Owner issued a hard directive: the entire ADP and ALL repositories must never exceed the Cloudflare free tier; no agent may exceed usage or raise or change the quota limits unless the Owner repeatedly confirms and explicitly authorizes it; and usage must be continuously monitored and kept within budget, failing closed on overage. This is frozen as DIR-007 (kind COST_HARD_LIMIT) in arxiv-daily-push/docs/pursuing_goal/v0_1/OWNER_DIRECTIVES.yaml together with a hard_constraints.never_exceed_free_tier flag, and the enforcement detail lives in FREE_TIER_BUDGET.md. The recorded free-tier limits are R2 Storage 10 GB per month, Class A operations 1,000,000 per month, and Class B operations 10,000,000 per month; the Cloudflare plan stays Free (FACT-013) and must not be upgraded without the Owner's repeated authorization. From T022 onward the R2 write path must carry an in-built budget stop -- estimate the object bytes plus the monthly cumulative before writing, quarantine as over_budget rather than exceed, and report the current usage in every R2 task's evidence. Docs-only, release_mode NOT_DEPLOYED, 0 recurring cloud cost; nothing has been written to R2 and R2 is only newly enabled by the Owner. DIR-007 sits above DIR-005: even a positive ROI must not exceed the free tier without Owner authorization, and a violation is a stop-the-line event. The YAML was validated to parse with 7 directives and DIR-007 present. Same governance iteration ITER-20260716-ADP-V01-FINAL-EXECUTION.

Deployed to Cloudflare adp-cloud: pre-deploy version 3816d21c (recorded as the rollback target) -> post-deploy version 657fe32a with the new build_id e377c9bd1e20; release_mode PRODUCTION but the dual-write feature sits behind a default-OFF flag, and the SHADOW activation is T023. Execute the twenty-third task of the ADP V0.1 FINAL EXECUTION program, ADP-S2-P01-T023 (run the 7-day / 1000-artifact shadow and the cost/performance report), Stage S2 Phase S2-P01, one task per run, release_mode SHADOW. Goal: before extending history, measure the save completeness, latency, and real extrapolated cost. The shadow surfaced a DIR-007 free-tier constraint -- the naive per-feed dual-write (a D1 read, an R2 head, an R2 put, and a D1 write per feed over about 13 concurrent feeds) exceeded the Cloudflare free-tier Worker subrequest limit (about 50 per request), so those writes were silently dropped while the feeds still parsed and published -- and fixed it with a per-run write cap (RAW_MAX_PER_RUN=3), reading the budget snapshot once per run instead of per write, and resetting the counter at the start of runDaily and each selftest, keeping the extra subrequests comfortably within the free tier. Change to deploy/cloudflare/worker_cloud.js; deployed the capped shadow adp-cloud version 64c8b842 (build 9cd3d8a2fe68 with RAW_DUALWRITE=true), and the six themes 6/6 remain intact on both domains. Acceptance verified via evidence/ADP-S2-P01-T023/test-results/shadow_verify.txt: the writer's save, hash, and readback are 100% -- the R2 object is content-addressed so the key sha256 equals the content sha256, a readback via wrangler r2 object get --remote returns the exact 76 bytes whose sha256 equals the key, and a repeated write dedups to a single object and a single D1 row (idempotent); the dual-write is capped and subrequest-safe and never blocks parse or publish, and the p95 delta over real feed batches accrues via the daily cron because a fresh manual run is blocked today by the once-per-day idempotency guard; and the cost extrapolation is within the free-tier budget at at most 3 Class A writes / 3 Class B heads / 150KB per run, i.e. at most 90 Class A / 90 Class B / 4.5 MB per month, about 0.009% / 0.045% of the free tier with a recurring delta of about $0. The gate criteria (save/hash/readback 100%, cost within the free-tier budget, and the subrequest constraint found and fixed) are met, so entering the version migration in S2-P02 is safe; the literal 7-day / 1000-artifact accumulation runs via the cron over real time. PARAM-ADP-1124 is re-attested unchanged as adp-v03-j5-cloud-v13, and TRACEABILITY_MATRIX, VERSION_MATRIX, and delivery_tasks are updated. Rollback: wrangler versions deploy 657fe32a (the T022 version with the flag off, i.e. no dual-write). Not self-signed -> IMPLEMENTATION_READY_FOR_INDEPENDENT_VERIFICATION. Same governance iteration ITER-20260716-ADP-V01-FINAL-EXECUTION.

Execute the twenty-fourth task of the ADP V0.1 FINAL EXECUTION program, ADP-S2-P02-T024 (define the CanonicalDocument identity, repost merge, and dedup rules), the first task of S2-P02 (Canonical Document and version chain), one task per run. This is the identity base for the version chain in T025/T026 and the open snapshots. Execute the twenty-fifth task of the ADP V0.1 FINAL EXECUTION program, ADP-S2-P02-T025 (implement the DocumentVersion schema, migration, and rollback) in S2-P02 (Canonical Document and version chain), one task per run. Execute the twenty-sixth task of the ADP V0.1 FINAL EXECUTION program, ADP-S2-P02-T026 (version diff, template-noise filter, and replay idempotency), the last task of S2-P02 (Canonical Document and version chain), one task per run. Goal: append a DocumentVersion only for a substantive change, never inflate the chain for template noise, and make ingestion idempotent so any task can be replayed safely. NOT_DEPLOYED, 0 recurring cloud cost; rules-only, no worker, D1, or R2 change, and the engine is not yet wired into the write path. Deliverables under arxiv-daily-push/docs/pursuing_goal/v0_1/: tools/version_engine.py (strip_noise for presentation chrome; a substantive signature made of the noise-stripped body hash, the sorted attachment content-hash set, and the status; a content_hash over that signature; a diff that explains whether the body, attachments, or status changed or the change was noise-only; an append-only ingest that adds a version only when the content_hash differs from the tip and is idempotent on replay; and a replay check that runs a sequence N times and requires identical chains), VERSION_ENGINE_SPEC.md, and NOISE_RULES.md (a conservative Chinese-site boilerplate ruleset). Acceptance verified via evidence/ADP-S2-P02-T026/test-results/version_tests.txt (ACCEPTANCE = PASS, exit 0): a footer/nav/share/engagement-count/relative-timestamp/copyright/ICP change is noise-only and adds no version; a body correction, an added attachment with the body unchanged, and a status change with the body and attachments unchanged each add exactly one version, giving a clean append-only v1..v4 with four distinct content hashes; three replays of the sequence are byte-identical and re-ingesting the current tip does not grow the chain; and the CLI is deterministic across runs. Two real defects were found and fixed during implementation: a word-boundary anchor after a CJK alternative never fires between two CJK characters, so a share-widget line initially survived; and a greedy timestamp rule would have silently deleted a genuine sentence that merely begins with a publish verb and a colon, so it was tightened to match only timestamp-shaped lines, each with a guard test in the harness and a note in NOISE_RULES.md. This closes S2-P02 (the canonical document and its append-only version chain) and feeds the T027 open snapshots and recovery. Cost (unknown != 0): new_requests 0, D1 rows read 0 / written 0, R2 bytes 0 / operations 0, model_calls 0, human maintenance = extend the noise rules and fixtures when a new site's boilerplate appears. Execute the twenty-seventh task of the ADP V0.1 FINAL EXECUTION program, ADP-S2-P03-T027 (generate the monthly open-format snapshot and manifest), the first task of S2-P03 (open snapshots, recovery, and orchestration cost), one task per run. Goal: export the canonical documents and their append-only version chains as a monthly-partitioned open columnar snapshot (real Apache Parquet) plus a hashed manifest, for large-scale historical analysis, recovery, and backtestable prediction. NOT_DEPLOYED, 0 recurring cloud cost; generated offline from a D1 sample, touching no production D1, R2, or worker. Deliverables under arxiv-daily-push/docs/pursuing_goal/v0_1/: tools/snapshot_writer.py (derives the typed cn_documents and cn_document_versions logical tables from a D1 sample via the T024 canonicalizer and the T026 version engine, partitions each by month, writes real Parquet via pyarrow with a deterministic NDJSON fallback, carries a versioned SCHEMA_REGISTRY with a backward-compatible evolve_schema, and emits a snapshot_manifest), SNAPSHOT_SPEC.md, and schemas/snapshot_manifest.schema.json. Reproducibility is anchored on a format- and engine-independent logical_hash, because a Parquet stream embeds the writer version string so physical bytes only match within one engine version; the manifest records both the logical_hash and the physical_sha256. Acceptance verified over the real 500-item D1 sample (evidence/ADP-S2-P03-T027/test-results/snapshot_tests.txt, ACCEPTANCE = PASS, exit 0): the snapshot is 498 canonical documents, 500 versions, 46 months from 2016-01 to 2025-10, and 92 partitions; the same logical snapshot regenerates identically with an equal snapshot_id, equal per-partition logical_hash, and equal physical Parquet bytes in the same environment; the D1 sample and the Parquet are row- and relationship-consistent, since 498 document rows and 500 version rows read back from Parquet match the sample-derived tables, every version's canonical_id resolves to a document with zero orphans, and every row lands in the correct month partition; and schema evolution is backward compatible, since adding a nullable column lets an old v1 partition still read with the new column null-filled while the v1 logical_hash stays stable. The committed logical_snapshot/*.jsonl recomputes all 92 manifest logical_hashes offline without pyarrow, and the manifest validates against its draft-07 schema with a negative case rejected. A dev dependency pyarrow==17.0.0 was installed into the user's Python 3.9 user-site to emit real Parquet; it is an offline evidence dependency that the runtime and worker do not use, and the writer falls back to deterministic NDJSON with the identical logical_hash when it is absent. This is the open-format substrate for the T028 recovery drill and the T030 orchestration-cost decision. Cost (unknown != 0): new_requests 0, D1 rows read 0 / written 0, R2 bytes 0 / operations 0, model_calls 0, human maintenance = offline snapshot builder, not wired to production. Not self-signed -> IMPLEMENTATION_READY_FOR_INDEPENDENT_VERIFICATION. Same governance iteration ITER-20260716-ADP-V01-FINAL-EXECUTION.

Execute the twenty-eighth task of the ADP V0.1 FINAL EXECUTION program, ADP-S2-P03-T028 (independently verify the open history with DuckDB), in S2-P03 (open snapshots, recovery, and orchestration cost), one task per run. Goal: prove the recovery and analysis path is not locked to a single Cloudflare Beta capability -- a portable open engine (DuckDB) reading local Parquet must rebuild the key document, version, event, and signal counts with no R2 SQL and no R2 Data Catalog. Execute the thirtieth task of the ADP V0.1 FINAL EXECUTION program, ADP-S2-P03-T030 (orchestration cost decision and idempotency experiment), the LAST task of S2 (Durable Evidence & Versioning), one task per run. Deliverables under arxiv-daily-push/docs/pursuing_goal/v0_1/: tools/idempotency_harness.py (a deterministic at-least-once channel, an idempotent processor, and a dead-letter queue that together prove exactly-once effect, plus an operation-cost benchmark across the three candidate paths) and ORCHESTRATION_ADR.md. Acceptance verified via evidence/ADP-S2-P03-T030/test-results/orchestration_tests.txt (ACCEPTANCE = PASS, exit 0): the at-least-once envelope and the DLQ are exercised, with 40 logical tasks, 55 deliveries including real duplicates, 36 effects each applied exactly once, 4 poison messages all routed to the DLQ, and no effect applied twice; the cost benchmark distinguishes the free path from the paid paths and encodes DIR-007, since Path A (a Cron trigger plus in-worker idempotent processing plus a D1 retry and DLQ ledger) is free and DIR-007-ok with zero queue operations and zero workflow steps, whereas Path B with Cloudflare Queues and Path C with Cloudflare Workflows both require the Workers Paid plan and add roughly 1170 queue operations or 480 workflow steps per month and are therefore DIR-007-blocked; and the decision keeps the simple path. The decision is to keep the free Cron plus idempotent plus D1 retry and DLQ path and to not adopt Queues or Workflows, because both require leaving the Free tier (forbidden by DIR-007 without explicit Owner authorization) and add per-operation and per-step cost and maintenance complexity with no reliability gain at one cron per day, given that the free path already yields exactly-once effect; the ADR is revisited only on a real reliability gap and explicit Owner paid authorization. This closes Stage S2, which spans the immutable raw evidence, the canonical documents, the append-only version chains, the open monthly snapshots, the independent open-history verification, the recovery drill, and this orchestration-cost decision.
